content:
Key Findings

  • Pendle Borough Council's total analysed spending across five financial years, from 2021/22 to 2025/26, amounted to £129.1M.
  • The Finance department was the highest spending department, accounting for £34.6M of the council's expenditure.
  • Spending by the Finance department represented 26.8% of the council's total analysed spend.
  • The second highest spending department was Financial Services, with an expenditure of £26.8M.
  • Operational Services ranked third, spending £15.6M over the period.

Overview of Pendle's Financial Landscape

Pendle Borough Council's spending data, covering the financial years 2021/22 to 2025/26, reveals a total expenditure of £129.1M. This comprehensive dataset offers insight into how public funds are allocated across various departments and services within the district council. A significant portion of this spending is attributed to the Finance department, which recorded an expenditure of £34.6M during this period. This figure positions Finance as the largest spending department within the council's operations.

The Finance department's spend of £34.6M represents a substantial 26.8% of the council's overall analysed spending. This highlights the critical role and financial commitment associated with managing the council's financial affairs, including budgeting, accounting, treasury management, and financial planning. Understanding these figures is crucial for residents to grasp the scale of financial operations at Pendle Borough Council and how resources are prioritised.

Top Spending Departments at Pendle Borough Council

An analysis of Pendle Borough Council's spending data shows a clear distribution of funds across its various departments and services. The Finance department leads this distribution, but other key areas also command significant portions of the council's budget. The following table details the top ten departments by expenditure, providing a clear picture of where Pendle Borough Council's funds are primarily directed.

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 Finance £34.6M 26.8%
2 Financial Services £26.8M 20.8%
3 Operational Services £15.6M 12.1%
4 Engineering & Special Projects £14.5M 11.2%
5 Economic Growth £12.7M 9.8%
6 Policy & Commissioning £6.6M 5.1%
7 Housing & Environmental Health £5.2M 4.0%
8 Property Services £3.3M 2.5%
9 Property & Engineering £3.1M 2.4%
10 Parks & Recreation Services £1.9M 1.4%

Beyond the leading Finance department, the Financial Services department is the second highest spender, with an expenditure of £26.8M, representing 20.8% of the total. This suggests a distinction in financial functions, with Finance potentially covering broader strategic financial management and Financial Services focusing on specific operational aspects. Together, these two financial departments account for a substantial portion of the council's overall spending, highlighting the extensive resources dedicated to financial administration and related services.

Operational Services ranks as the third largest spending department, with an expenditure of £15.6M, equating to 12.1% of the total. This category typically encompasses a wide range of essential day-to-day services that keep the council functioning and serving its residents. Following closely is Engineering & Special Projects, which spent £14.5M, or 11.2% of the total. This indicates significant investment in infrastructure, development, and specific initiatives within the borough.

Understanding the Scale of Financial Operations

The combined spending of the Finance department at £34.6M and Financial Services at £26.8M demonstrates a considerable investment in the financial backbone of Pendle Borough Council. These figures are significantly higher than other key service areas such as Economic Growth, which spent £12.7M, and Housing & Environmental Health, with an expenditure of £5.2M. The substantial allocation to financial functions underscores their foundational importance to all other council activities, ensuring fiscal responsibility and the proper management of public money.

The data covers a five-year period, from 2021/22 to 2025/26, providing a long-term perspective on spending patterns. While the data does not allow for year-on-year trend analysis, the aggregated figures offer a robust snapshot of the council's financial priorities over half a decade. The consistent high expenditure in financial departments reflects the ongoing need for robust financial controls, auditing, and strategic financial planning to support all services delivered to the residents of Pendle.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Pendle Borough Council about the specific breakdown of spending within the Finance department.
  • Attend council committee meetings, such as those related to finance or resources, which are usually open to the public, to hear discussions on council spending.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see details of agreements made with suppliers for financial services or other departmental needs.
  • Write to your local councillor about the level of spending by the Finance department and ask for more details on what this expenditure covers.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Pendle Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Pendle Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
